dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7276 IH: To impose a 30 percent duty on sheep products and lamb products from Australia or New Zealand.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Amodei of Nevada introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To impose a 30 percent duty on sheep products and lamb products from Australia or New Zealand.

section.header: Imposition of duty on sheep products and lamb products from Australia or New Zealand

subsection.text: Beginning on and after the date that is 30 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, in addition to each other applicable duty, the President shall impose a 30 percent duty on each sheep product and lamb product originating from Australia or New Zealand.

paragraph.text: The term lamb means meat, other than mutton, produced from sheep.
The term lamb product means products produced in whole or in part from lamb, including pelts.
The term sheep means ovine animals of any age, including ewes and rams.
The term sheep product means products produced in whole or in part from sheep, including wool and products containing wool.
The term wool means the fiber from the fleece of a sheep.
